sourceShortSummaries: At the Leviathan, Wazir shares The Ciphered Scroll and the party connects Hamri's recent deaths to Gazankoa, Airion, Nimessa, and the stolen Sentient Ocean.
sourceLongSummaries: The party gathers at the Leviathan while Wazir walks through The Ciphered Scroll: its author sought to restore his dead wife Nadine, sacrificed people whose bodies filled with water, chased legends of Airion the Mistspeaker, found a ring tied to Nimessa, and came to Hamri after calling on Gazankoa. The group debates resurrection, gatza visions, Medin, Airion's ruined tower in the North Cliffs above the sea cave, and the living water before deciding to rest and search the tower in the morning.

sourceEntries.title: Initial Scroll Discussion
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The session opens with one continuous Leviathan discussion: Wazir explains the deciphered scroll, the party connects Gazankoa, gatza, Airion, Nimessa, Medin, Hamri, and the living water, then decides that the tower should be searched after a night's rest.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: At the Leviathan, Wazir shares The Ciphered Scroll and the party connects Hamri's recent deaths to Gazankoa, Airion, Nimessa, and the stolen Sentient Ocean.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The party gathers at the Leviathan while Wazir walks through The Ciphered Scroll: its author sought to restore his dead wife Nadine, sacrificed people whose bodies filled with water, chased legends of Airion the Mistspeaker, found a ring tied to Nimessa, and came to Hamri after calling on Gazankoa. The group debates resurrection, gatza visions, Medin, Airion's ruined tower in the North Cliffs above the sea cave, and the living water before deciding to rest and search the tower in the morning.

locationHints.context: the Leviathan Inn, where the party compares notes over Wazir's translation
locationHints.notes: The opening discussion stays at the inn and ends with the group resting before the morning search.

npcDetails.name: Azar the Lost
npcDetails.notes: His account drives the investigation into the recent deaths and the strange water.

npcDetails.name: Gazankoa
npcDetails.context: voice or power named in the scroll
npcDetails.notes: The party treats Gazankoa as the force behind the scroll author's visions and sacrifices.

npcDetails.name: Nadine
npcDetails.notes: Azar's desire to bring her back is the emotional center of the scroll.

npcDetails.name: Nimessa
npcDetails.notes: The scroll says she was compelled to steal the Sentient Ocean.

npcDetails.name: Airion
npcDetails.notes: Airion's tower, ring, and water magic become the party's next investigative thread.

npcDetails.name: Medin
npcDetails.context: place or term named in the scroll
npcDetails.notes: Wazir flags Medin as part of the scroll's chain of clues.

itemDetails.name: The Ciphered Scroll
itemDetails.notes: Wazir has translated it and reads its contents to the party.

itemDetails.name: Sentient Ocean
itemDetails.notes: The recovered living water appears to be the object Azar wanted Nimessa to steal.

itemDetails.name: Airion's ring
itemDetails.notes: The scroll links the ring to summoning or commanding Nimessa.

sourceShortSummaries: The party searches Airion's ruined tower in the North Cliffs, finds signs of recent occupation, and confirms that an old watery illusion still clings to the site.
The party fans out across Hamri and learns that a haggard old man has been using the tower, smoking gatza, and asking questions about lead.
At the market and in afternoon errands, the party gets Benat's history of Airion, Ovina's lead-box confession, and Sofi's curt message about strange ships.
The party consults Yaz, an old ocean-watcher, before resting for the evening.
sourceLongSummaries: The next morning the group reaches Airion's ruined tower in the North Cliffs, above the sea cave, and discovers that a newer watchtower shell sits on much older stonework. Inside, the place throws them into a convincing Plane of Water illusion with dolphins, black-water tendrils, and Airion's water-commanding incantation before the image fades back to the shallow flooded chamber; the evidence of recent habitation sends them back toward town informants.

After leaving the tower, Ryu and Trok go to Jaeson while the others check Samar, Reliah, and Orden for local gossip. The combined reports point to a worn older man who appeared only recently, hid signs of living in the tower, left fish remains and the smell of gatza, had ties to the Drowned Flats deaths, and had been asking around about lead before the group prepares to reconvene at the market.

The party regroups at the fish market after lunch, compares the morning's clues, and presses Benat for the older story of Airion's tower, Hamri's hidden weak spots, and the living water. They split again: Kaleho and Trok persuade Ovina to show the nearly finished lead-lined box and the dismantled gold-and-sapphire ring Azar paid with, while Wazir and Ryu climb to Glitter Cliff and receive only a formal message that Sofi knows of no strange ship and wants any sightings reported for fees and duties.

Wazir tests Ovina's ring and box, and Yaz gives Ryu and Trok more context for the living ocean and the Splinters. Back at the Leviathan, the party plans to let one person approach Azar while the others hide nearby.

sourceEntries.title: Searching Airion's Tower
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The transcript advances to the next morning as the party reaches the North Cliffs tower, inspects the hidden older stonework and planar illusion within, and leaves only once they decide the best next move is to ask town contacts for context.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party searches Airion's ruined tower in the North Cliffs, finds signs of recent occupation, and confirms that an old watery illusion still clings to the site.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The next morning the group reaches Airion's ruined tower in the North Cliffs, above the sea cave, and discovers that a newer watchtower shell sits on much older stonework. Inside, the place throws them into a convincing Plane of Water illusion with dolphins, black-water tendrils, and Airion's water-commanding incantation before the image fades back to the shallow flooded chamber; the evidence of recent habitation sends them back toward town informants.

locationHints.notes: The beat stays focused on the tower, its older foundation, and the lingering water magic within.

npcDetails.notes: His name appears in the incantation tied to the water illusion.

npcDetails.name: recent tower occupant
npcDetails.context: unidentified person who had been living in the tower
npcDetails.notes: The party finds signs that someone has used the ruin in recent weeks.

itemDetails.name: Ring of Water Walking
itemDetails.notes: Kaleho uses the ring while testing the tower's water illusion.

itemDetails.name: Cloak of the Manta Ray
itemDetails.notes: Wazir offers it as a practical response to Trok being in real water beneath the illusion.

sourceEntries.title: Morning Town Informants
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: After leaving the tower, the party splits into parallel morning interviews with Jaeson, Samar, Reliah, and Orden, gathering evidence about the occupied tower, the collapsed Drowned Flats shack, gatza addicts, and the suspicious request for a lead-lined box before regrouping for the market.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party fans out across Hamri and learns that a haggard old man has been using the tower, smoking gatza, and asking questions about lead.
sourceEntries.longSummary: After leaving the tower, Ryu and Trok go to Jaeson while the others check Samar, Reliah, and Orden for local gossip. The combined reports point to a worn older man who appeared only recently, hid signs of living in the tower, left fish remains and the smell of gatza, had ties to the Drowned Flats deaths, and had been asking around about lead before the group prepares to reconvene at the market.

locationHints.context: split inquiries around the north face, the Drowned Flats, and the docks
locationHints.notes: The party moves between local informants but stays within Hamri.

npcDetails.name: Jaeson
npcDetails.notes: He saw signs of someone using the tower recently.

npcDetails.name: Samar
npcDetails.context: orphan informant in the Drowned Flats
npcDetails.notes: She helps confirm local rumors around the recent deaths and the stranger's movements.

npcDetails.name: Reliah
npcDetails.context: orphan informant in the Drowned Flats
npcDetails.notes: She adds neighborhood detail from the same poor quarter hit by the disappearances.

npcDetails.name: Orden
npcDetails.notes: He contributes what he knows about the strange boat and waterfront movements.

npcDetails.name: haggard old man
npcDetails.notes: The informants' reports are later understood to point to Azar.

itemDetails.name: gatza
itemDetails.notes: The tower and the old man's behavior are repeatedly connected to gatza use.

itemDetails.name: lead
itemDetails.notes: The party learns that the suspect was asking about lead before commissioning the box.

sourceEntries.title: Market and Afternoon Leads
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The party reconvenes at the fish market after lunch, gets Benat's account of Airion and Hamri's planar weak spots, then follows afternoon leads: Kaleho and Trok question Ovina about the lead-lined box while Wazir and Ryu seek information from Sofi before everyone meets again at the Shark's Dock.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: At the market and in afternoon errands, the party gets Benat's history of Airion, Ovina's lead-box confession, and Sofi's curt message about strange ships.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The party regroups at the fish market after lunch, compares the morning's clues, and presses Benat for the older story of Airion's tower, Hamri's hidden weak spots, and the living water. They split again: Kaleho and Trok persuade Ovina to show the nearly finished lead-lined box and the dismantled gold-and-sapphire ring Azar paid with, while Wazir and Ryu climb to Glitter Cliff and receive only a formal message that Sofi knows of no strange ship and wants any sightings reported for fees and duties.

locationHints.context: the fish market, Ovina's smithy near Shark's Dock, and Sofi's house on Glitter Cliff
locationHints.notes: The beat condenses the market regroup and two parallel afternoon leads.

npcDetails.name: Benat
npcDetails.context: lizardfolk liaison and teller of local history
npcDetails.notes: He explains Airion's old tower and Hamri's deeper magical context.

npcDetails.name: Ovina
npcDetails.notes: She admits that the old man paid her to make a sealed lead-lined box.

npcDetails.name: Sofi
npcDetails.context: wealthy shipbuilding magnate on Glitter Cliff
npcDetails.notes: She declines to meet directly and sends a polished message through her household.

npcDetails.name: Sofi's servant
npcDetails.notes: He carries Sofi's reply to Wazir and Ryu.

npcDetails.name: Azar the Lost
npcDetails.notes: The party increasingly treats him as the man behind the scroll and the box commission.

npcDetails.name: Airion
npcDetails.context: ancient wizard tied to Hamri's old tower lore
npcDetails.notes: Benat's account deepens the party's sense of Airion's importance.

itemDetails.name: lead-lined box
itemDetails.notes: Ovina shows the sealed box Azar ordered, sized slightly larger than a breadbox.

itemDetails.name: gold ring with sapphires
itemDetails.notes: Azar paid Ovina with the ring, which she has taken apart.

itemDetails.name: phantom boat
itemDetails.notes: The strange ship remains the subject of the inquiry at Glitter Cliff and Shark's Dock.

sourceEntries.title: Yaz and Evening Plan
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The group regathers at the Shark's Dock, consolidates what it knows about the phantom boat and Azar's motives, visits Yaz for sunset lore about the living ocean and the Splinters, then returns to the Leviathan after dark and settles on the next morning's cautious interception plan before resting.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party consults Yaz, an old ocean-watcher, before resting for the evening.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Wazir tests Ovina's ring and box, and Yaz gives Ryu and Trok more context for the living ocean and the Splinters. Back at the Leviathan, the party plans to let one person approach Azar while the others hide nearby.

locationHints.context: Shark's Dock, Yaz's tower, and the Leviathan Inn
locationHints.notes: The beat begins with a late-afternoon regroup, includes the evening visit to Yaz, and ends with the night plan before rest.

npcDetails.name: Yaz
npcDetails.notes: His lore about the living ocean helps the party interpret the Sentient Ocean.

npcDetails.name: Ovina
npcDetails.context: smith holding the ring and box
npcDetails.notes: Wazir inspects the ring and lead-lined box at her shop.

npcDetails.name: Azar the Lost
npcDetails.notes: The group expects him to return for the finished box.

npcDetails.name: Gazankoa
npcDetails.context: suspected voice behind the Blood Knife
npcDetails.notes: Wazir argues that Gazankoa is the real danger behind Azar's actions.

npcDetails.name: Nimessa
npcDetails.context: marid tied to the theft of the Sentient Ocean
npcDetails.notes: The party reasons that she was compelled and angered by the ring.

npcDetails.name: Airion
npcDetails.context: owner of the ring and water lore
npcDetails.notes: The ring and Hamri's weak points keep pointing back to Airion.

itemDetails.name: lead-lined box
itemDetails.notes: The box becomes the centerpiece of the plan to contain the Blood Knife and catch Azar.

itemDetails.name: gold ring with sapphires
itemDetails.notes: Wazir finds no active magic in it, though the party suspects it was once Airion's ring.

itemDetails.name: Blood Knife
itemDetails.notes: The party suspects the Blood Knife is carrying Gazankoa's influence.

itemDetails.name: Sentient Ocean
itemDetails.notes: The living water remains the motive for Azar's ritual and Nimessa's theft.

itemDetails.name: phantom boat
itemDetails.notes: The party expects Azar to use it when he comes for the box.

sourceShortSummaries: At dawn, the party spots Azar's phantom boat, confronts him after he collects the box, and sees the parley turn into an imminent fight.
Azar erupts into black tentacles, runs for the phantom boat, and punishes the party's first attempts to restrain him.
The fight turns after Trok is revived, breaks Azar's concentration, drops him unconscious, and leaves Kaleho to finish the drowned servant.
After the fight, the party stabilizes Azar and contains the dockside chaos.
The party hears Azar's full tale after tending Shark's Dock.
The Splinters expedition clears Azar's lair and recovers his remaining evidence.
sourceLongSummaries: The next morning the party stages itself around Shark's Dock after Blue spots a fast ship rounding the point. Azar arrives in oilskin cloaks, collects the lead-lined box from Ovina, and tries to leave; Ryu blocks him with questions about the tower, the deaths, and the Sentient Ocean while invisible Wazir uses Nadine's name to unsettle him, and the old man admits he is Azar and that Gazankoa is helping him save his family before a clearly nefarious invitation onto the boat makes the party draw weapons.

As the party attacks, Azar throws black tentacles across the dock, takes spear and melee hits, and bolts for the phantom boat while his drowned servant rises from the vessel dripping seawater. Trok drags Azar into the water and Kaleho lands a hard crossbow shot, but Azar's defenses and whispers keep him dangerous; the box is dropped in the boat, the vessel is pushed away, the tentacles keep crushing Ryu and Wazir, and Trok's huge underwater assault is answered by Armor of Agathys cold that knocks him unconscious.

With Trok unconscious in the water and Ryu restrained, the party scrambles to recover: Ryu's healing spirit brings Trok back up, Wazir's shatter destroys Azar's cold armor, and Azar answers with lightning that kills a nearby townsfolk and batters Ryu and Kaleho. Trok heals himself, lashes out with Eldritch Maul crocodile strikes, finally breaks the tentacles, and knocks Azar unconscious; Kaleho then shoots the remaining drowned servant down so Wazir can rush to heal Azar before he dies.
After the fight at Shark's Dock, Wazir heals Azar and the party binds him while beginning to recover from their injuries and the chaos around the dock.
After recovering from their injuries, calming the civilians on Shark's Dock, and tending the woman killed in Azar's lightning blast, the party hears Azar's full tale from Azar.

An evening expedition to the Splinters quickly dispatches two more drowned zombies and finds Sefa, a poor older gatza addict from the Drowned Flats who lived alone; the party recovers Airion's annotated books, two Drankorian works and one Elvish work, plus the Blood Knife, which they lock in the lead-lined box, the brass bowl, and scattered coins paid as restitution to Sefa and the families of the dead who can be found.

sourceEntries.title: Dawn Interception Setup
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The transcript moves into the next morning as the party waits for Azar, spots the phantom boat, arranges the attempted parley, and watches the conversation fail when Azar's invitation to Ryu proves openly hostile and initiative is called.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: At dawn, the party spots Azar's phantom boat, confronts him after he collects the box, and sees the parley turn into an imminent fight.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The next morning the party stages itself around Shark's Dock after Blue spots a fast ship rounding the point. Azar arrives in oilskin cloaks, collects the lead-lined box from Ovina, and tries to leave; Ryu blocks him with questions about the tower, the deaths, and the Sentient Ocean while invisible Wazir uses Nadine's name to unsettle him, and the old man admits he is Azar and that Gazankoa is helping him save his family before a clearly nefarious invitation onto the boat makes the party draw weapons.

locationHints.context: Shark's Dock outside Ovina's shop and beside Azar's phantom boat
locationHints.notes: The beat covers the dawn stakeout and failed parley before initiative resolves into active combat.

npcDetails.name: Azar the Lost
npcDetails.context: old man trying to reach his boat with the box
npcDetails.notes: He identifies himself, insists he is trying to save his family, and becomes openly hostile when stopped.

npcDetails.name: Ovina
npcDetails.context: smith who opens early for the box handoff
npcDetails.notes: Azar collects the finished box from her shop.

npcDetails.name: Nadine
npcDetails.notes: Wazir uses her name in a whispered magical message to shake Azar.

npcDetails.name: Gazankoa
npcDetails.context: power Azar says is helping him
npcDetails.notes: Azar says Gazankoa can bring back the lost dead.

npcDetails.name: drowned servant
npcDetails.notes: A figure is present on the boat before the combat reveals it clearly.

itemDetails.name: lead-lined box
itemDetails.notes: Azar carries it under one arm as he leaves Ovina's shop.

itemDetails.name: phantom boat
itemDetails.notes: The boat is hard to keep in sight and sits ready at Shark's Dock.

itemDetails.name: Sentient Ocean
itemDetails.notes: Ryu presses Azar about the living water during the attempted conversation.

itemDetails.name: Blood Knife
itemDetails.notes: Ryu asks whether the Blood Knife is speaking to Azar.

sourceEntries.title: Tentacles at the Dock
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The dock fight begins as Azar drops black tentacles, runs for the phantom boat, and the party tries to stop him. The combat phase covers the scramble over the boat, Ryu's restraint, Trok grappling Azar underwater, and Armor of Agathys punishing the first melee attempts until Trok is knocked unconscious.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Azar erupts into black tentacles, runs for the phantom boat, and punishes the party's first attempts to restrain him.
sourceEntries.longSummary: As the party attacks, Azar throws black tentacles across the dock, takes spear and melee hits, and bolts for the phantom boat while his drowned servant rises from the vessel dripping seawater. Trok drags Azar into the water and Kaleho lands a hard crossbow shot, but Azar's defenses and whispers keep him dangerous; the box is dropped in the boat, the vessel is pushed away, the tentacles keep crushing Ryu and Wazir, and Trok's huge underwater assault is answered by Armor of Agathys cold that knocks him unconscious.

locationHints.context: Shark's Dock, the water beside it, and Azar's phantom boat
locationHints.notes: The fight moves between the dock, the boat, and the water.

npcDetails.name: Azar the Lost
npcDetails.notes: He casts black tentacles, keeps concentration through repeated hits, and uses cold armor and whispers to punish the party.

npcDetails.name: drowned servant
npcDetails.notes: It rises from the boat and joins the fight against Wazir and the others.

itemDetails.name: lead-lined box
itemDetails.notes: Azar drops it in the bottom of the phantom boat during the fight.

itemDetails.name: phantom boat
itemDetails.notes: The party tries to keep Azar from escaping on it and eventually pushes it away from the dock.

mainEnemies.name: Azar the Lost
mainEnemies.notes: Primary hostile spellcaster trying to escape with the box.

mainEnemies.name: drowned servant
mainEnemies.notes: Waterlogged undead ally supporting Azar from the boat and dock.

combatHints.notes: The dock fight begins after the failed parley and escalates across the dock, water, and phantom boat.

sourceEntries.title: Azar Falls
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The second combat phase starts with Trok unconscious in the water, then turns when healing spirit, ranged fire, and Trok's magic bring Azar down and Kaleho finishes the drowned servant. The beat ends when the enemies are defeated and the party can stabilize Azar and Trok.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The fight turns after Trok is revived, breaks Azar's concentration, drops him unconscious, and leaves Kaleho to finish the drowned servant.
sourceEntries.longSummary: With Trok unconscious in the water and Ryu restrained, the party scrambles to recover: Ryu's healing spirit brings Trok back up, Wazir's shatter destroys Azar's cold armor, and Azar answers with lightning that kills a nearby townsfolk and batters Ryu and Kaleho. Trok heals himself, lashes out with Eldritch Maul crocodile strikes, finally breaks the tentacles, and knocks Azar unconscious; Kaleho then shoots the remaining drowned servant down so Wazir can rush to heal Azar before he dies.

locationHints.context: Shark's Dock and the adjacent water
locationHints.notes: This beat continues the same dockside combat through its resolution.

npcDetails.name: Azar the Lost
npcDetails.notes: He keeps fighting after leaving the water, but Trok's final barrage drops him unconscious.

npcDetails.name: drowned servant
npcDetails.notes: Kaleho kills it after Azar falls.

npcDetails.name: townsfolk bystander
npcDetails.notes: Azar's lightning blast kills one bystander during the fight.

itemDetails.name: healing spirit
itemDetails.notes: Ryu uses it to revive Trok before losing concentration.

itemDetails.name: Eldritch Maul tattoo
itemDetails.notes: Trok uses it to strike Azar from the water and end the fight.

itemDetails.name: lead-lined box
itemDetails.notes: It remains secured away on the boat while the combat resolves.

mainEnemies.name: Azar the Lost
mainEnemies.notes: The fight resolves when Trok knocks him unconscious.

mainEnemies.name: drowned servant
mainEnemies.notes: Kaleho finishes the servant after Azar falls.

combatHints.notes: This beat covers the crisis, recovery, and end of the dockside combat.

sourceEntries.title: Aftermath and Answers
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: After the fight, the party heals, stabilizes Azar, receives the narrated interrogation and lair recovery, secures the knife in the lead box, learns how Airion's ring, Nimessa, and the Sentient Ocean fit together, and carries the consequences through Azar's later departure from Hamri.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: After the fight, the party stabilizes Azar and contains the dockside chaos.
sourceEntries.longSummary: After the fight at Shark's Dock, Wazir heals Azar and the party binds him while beginning to recover from their injuries and the chaos around the dock.

locationHints.context: Shark's Dock, Azar's lair in the Splinters, and the following days around town
locationHints.notes: The beat starts at the dock after combat and compresses the narrated multi-day aftermath through 1747-08-09.

npcDetails.name: Azar the Lost
npcDetails.notes: He explains the murders, gives up the lair, and later leaves with Benat.

npcDetails.name: Sefa
npcDetails.notes: She was a poor older gatza addict who lived alone in the Drowned Flats.

npcDetails.name: drowned zombies
npcDetails.notes: Two more drowned zombies are quickly dispatched during the expedition to the Splinters.

npcDetails.name: Nadine
npcDetails.notes: Azar's failed ritual was meant to bring her back.

npcDetails.name: Gazankoa
npcDetails.context: source of the Blood Knife's influence
npcDetails.notes: Azar says the voice gave him the Blood Knife and lied that the killings could restore his wife.

npcDetails.name: Nimessa
npcDetails.notes: She stole the Sentient Ocean but was angry about being forced into it.

npcDetails.name: Airion
npcDetails.context: wizard whose annotated books and ring guided Azar
npcDetails.notes: His lore led Azar toward the Sentient Ocean and Hamri's water-plane weak spots.

npcDetails.name: Benat
npcDetails.notes: Azar leaves with him for the lizardfolk village on 1747-08-09.

itemDetails.name: Blood Knife
itemDetails.notes: The party recovers it from Azar's lair and locks it in the lead-lined box.

itemDetails.name: lead-lined box
itemDetails.notes: The box silences the Blood Knife once the party stores it inside.

itemDetails.name: Airion's Notes
itemDetails.notes: The party recovers three older books from Airion's library, annotated with Airion's glosses; two are Drankorian works and one is Elvish.

itemDetails.name: brass bowl
itemDetails.notes: Wazir identifies it as a bowl of commanding water elementals that must be filled with fresh ocean water and used immediately.

itemDetails.name: scattered coins
itemDetails.notes: The recovered coins are paid as restitution to Sefa and the families of the dead who can be found.

itemDetails.name: Airion's ring
itemDetails.notes: It was a one-use magic item that allowed Azar to command Nimessa.

itemDetails.name: Sentient Ocean
itemDetails.notes: Azar wanted living water because he believed ordinary seawater was insufficient for the ritual.

npcDetails.name: Nadine
npcDetails.notes: Azar's failed ritual was meant to bring her back.

npcDetails.name: Gazankoa
npcDetails.context: source of the Blood Knife's influence
npcDetails.notes: Azar says the voice gave him the Blood Knife and lied that the killings could restore his wife.

npcDetails.name: Nimessa
npcDetails.notes: She stole the Sentient Ocean but was angry about being forced into it.

npcDetails.name: Airion
npcDetails.context: wizard whose annotated books and ring guided Azar
npcDetails.notes: His lore led Azar toward the Sentient Ocean and Hamri's water-plane weak spots.

npcDetails.name: Benat
npcDetails.notes: Azar leaves with him for the lizardfolk village on 1747-08-09.

itemDetails.name: Blood Knife
itemDetails.notes: The party recovers it from Azar's lair and locks it in the lead-lined box.

itemDetails.name: lead-lined box
itemDetails.notes: The box silences the Blood Knife once the party stores it inside.

itemDetails.name: Airion's Notes
itemDetails.notes: The party recovers three older books from Airion's library, annotated with Airion's glosses; two are Drankorian works and one is Elvish.

itemDetails.name: brass bowl
itemDetails.notes: Wazir identifies it as a bowl of commanding water elementals that must be filled with fresh ocean water and used immediately.

itemDetails.name: scattered coins
itemDetails.notes: The recovered coins are paid as restitution to Sefa and the families of the dead who can be found.

itemDetails.name: Airion's ring
itemDetails.notes: It was a one-use magic item that allowed Azar to command Nimessa.

itemDetails.name: Sentient Ocean
itemDetails.notes: Azar wanted living water because he believed ordinary seawater was insufficient for the ritual.

sourceShortSummaries: Wazir identifies the recovered knife and brass bowl.
sourceLongSummaries: The next morning, Wazir identifies the items from Azar's lair: the Blood Knife remains shrouded in mystery and darkness beyond his magic, while the brass bowl is a bowl of commanding water elementals that must be filled with fresh ocean water and used immediately.

sourceEntries.title: Aftermath and Answers

recapBlocks.title: Initial Scroll Discussion
sourceShortSummaries: At the Leviathan, Wazir shares The Ciphered Scroll and the party connects Hamri's recent deaths to Gazankoa, Airion, Nimessa, and the stolen Sentient Ocean.
sourceLongSummaries: The party gathers at the Leviathan while Wazir walks through The Ciphered Scroll: its author sought to restore his dead wife Nadine, sacrificed people whose bodies filled with water, chased legends of Airion the Mistspeaker, found a ring tied to Nimessa, and came to Hamri after calling on Gazankoa. The group debates resurrection, gatza visions, Medin, Airion's ruined tower in the North Cliffs above the sea cave, and the living water before deciding to rest and search the tower in the morning.

sourceEntries.title: Initial Scroll Discussion
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The session opens with one continuous Leviathan discussion: Wazir explains the deciphered scroll, the party connects Gazankoa, gatza, Airion, Nimessa, Medin, Hamri, and the living water, then decides that the tower should be searched after a night's rest.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: At the Leviathan, Wazir shares The Ciphered Scroll and the party connects Hamri's recent deaths to Gazankoa, Airion, Nimessa, and the stolen Sentient Ocean.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The party gathers at the Leviathan while Wazir walks through The Ciphered Scroll: its author sought to restore his dead wife Nadine, sacrificed people whose bodies filled with water, chased legends of Airion the Mistspeaker, found a ring tied to Nimessa, and came to Hamri after calling on Gazankoa. The group debates resurrection, gatza visions, Medin, Airion's ruined tower in the North Cliffs above the sea cave, and the living water before deciding to rest and search the tower in the morning.

locationHints.context: the Leviathan Inn, where the party compares notes over Wazir's translation
locationHints.notes: The opening discussion stays at the inn and ends with the group resting before the morning search.

npcDetails.name: Azar the Lost
npcDetails.notes: His account drives the investigation into the recent deaths and the strange water.

npcDetails.name: Gazankoa
npcDetails.context: voice or power named in the scroll
npcDetails.notes: The party treats Gazankoa as the force behind the scroll author's visions and sacrifices.

npcDetails.name: Nadine
npcDetails.notes: Azar's desire to bring her back is the emotional center of the scroll.

npcDetails.name: Nimessa
npcDetails.notes: The scroll says she was compelled to steal the Sentient Ocean.

npcDetails.name: Airion
npcDetails.notes: Airion's tower, ring, and water magic become the party's next investigative thread.

npcDetails.name: Medin
npcDetails.context: place or term named in the scroll
npcDetails.notes: Wazir flags Medin as part of the scroll's chain of clues.

itemDetails.name: The Ciphered Scroll
itemDetails.notes: Wazir has translated it and reads its contents to the party.

itemDetails.name: Sentient Ocean
itemDetails.notes: The recovered living water appears to be the object Azar wanted Nimessa to steal.

itemDetails.name: Airion's ring
itemDetails.notes: The scroll links the ring to summoning or commanding Nimessa.

recapBlocks.title: Searching Airion's Tower
sourceShortSummaries: The party searches Airion's ruined tower in the North Cliffs, finds signs of recent occupation, and confirms that an old watery illusion still clings to the site.
sourceLongSummaries: The next morning the group reaches Airion's ruined tower in the North Cliffs, above the sea cave, and discovers that a newer watchtower shell sits on much older stonework. Inside, the place throws them into a convincing Plane of Water illusion with dolphins, black-water tendrils, and Airion's water-commanding incantation before the image fades back to the shallow flooded chamber; the evidence of recent habitation sends them back toward town informants.

sourceEntries.title: Searching Airion's Tower
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The transcript advances to the next morning as the party reaches the North Cliffs tower, inspects the hidden older stonework and planar illusion within, and leaves only once they decide the best next move is to ask town contacts for context.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party searches Airion's ruined tower in the North Cliffs, finds signs of recent occupation, and confirms that an old watery illusion still clings to the site.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The next morning the group reaches Airion's ruined tower in the North Cliffs, above the sea cave, and discovers that a newer watchtower shell sits on much older stonework. Inside, the place throws them into a convincing Plane of Water illusion with dolphins, black-water tendrils, and Airion's water-commanding incantation before the image fades back to the shallow flooded chamber; the evidence of recent habitation sends them back toward town informants.

locationHints.context: Airion's ruined tower above the sea cave
locationHints.notes: The beat stays focused on the tower, its older foundation, and the lingering water magic within.

npcDetails.name: Airion
npcDetails.context: former owner or source of the tower's old magic
npcDetails.notes: His name appears in the incantation tied to the water illusion.

npcDetails.name: recent tower occupant
npcDetails.context: unidentified person who had been living in the tower
npcDetails.notes: The party finds signs that someone has used the ruin in recent weeks.

itemDetails.name: Ring of Water Walking
itemDetails.notes: Kaleho uses the ring while testing the tower's water illusion.

itemDetails.name: Cloak of the Manta Ray
itemDetails.notes: Wazir offers it as a practical response to Trok being in real water beneath the illusion.

recapBlocks.title: Morning Town Informants
sourceShortSummaries: The party fans out across Hamri and learns that a haggard old man has been using the tower, smoking gatza, and asking questions about lead.
sourceLongSummaries: After leaving the tower, Ryu and Trok go to Jaeson while the others check Samar, Reliah, and Orden for local gossip. The combined reports point to a worn older man who appeared only recently, hid signs of living in the tower, left fish remains and the smell of gatza, had ties to the Drowned Flats deaths, and had been asking around about lead before the group prepares to reconvene at the market.

sourceEntries.title: Morning Town Informants
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: After leaving the tower, the party splits into parallel morning interviews with Jaeson, Samar, Reliah, and Orden, gathering evidence about the occupied tower, the collapsed Drowned Flats shack, gatza addicts, and the suspicious request for a lead-lined box before regrouping for the market.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party fans out across Hamri and learns that a haggard old man has been using the tower, smoking gatza, and asking questions about lead.
sourceEntries.longSummary: After leaving the tower, Ryu and Trok go to Jaeson while the others check Samar, Reliah, and Orden for local gossip. The combined reports point to a worn older man who appeared only recently, hid signs of living in the tower, left fish remains and the smell of gatza, had ties to the Drowned Flats deaths, and had been asking around about lead before the group prepares to reconvene at the market.

locationHints.context: split inquiries around the north face, the Drowned Flats, and the docks
locationHints.notes: The party moves between local informants but stays within Hamri.

npcDetails.name: Jaeson
npcDetails.notes: He saw signs of someone using the tower recently.

npcDetails.name: Samar
npcDetails.context: orphan informant in the Drowned Flats
npcDetails.notes: She helps confirm local rumors around the recent deaths and the stranger's movements.

npcDetails.name: Reliah
npcDetails.context: orphan informant in the Drowned Flats
npcDetails.notes: She adds neighborhood detail from the same poor quarter hit by the disappearances.

npcDetails.name: Orden
npcDetails.notes: He contributes what he knows about the strange boat and waterfront movements.

npcDetails.name: haggard old man
npcDetails.notes: The informants' reports are later understood to point to Azar.

itemDetails.name: gatza
itemDetails.notes: The tower and the old man's behavior are repeatedly connected to gatza use.

itemDetails.name: lead
itemDetails.notes: The party learns that the suspect was asking about lead before commissioning the box.

recapBlocks.title: Market and Afternoon Leads
sourceShortSummaries: At the market and in afternoon errands, the party gets Benat's history of Airion, Ovina's lead-box confession, and Sofi's curt message about strange ships.
sourceLongSummaries: The party regroups at the fish market after lunch, compares the morning's clues, and presses Benat for the older story of Airion's tower, Hamri's hidden weak spots, and the living water. They split again: Kaleho and Trok persuade Ovina to show the nearly finished lead-lined box and the dismantled gold-and-sapphire ring Azar paid with, while Wazir and Ryu climb to Glitter Cliff and receive only a formal message that Sofi knows of no strange ship and wants any sightings reported for fees and duties.

sourceEntries.title: Market and Afternoon Leads
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The party reconvenes at the fish market after lunch, gets Benat's account of Airion and Hamri's planar weak spots, then follows afternoon leads: Kaleho and Trok question Ovina about the lead-lined box while Wazir and Ryu seek information from Sofi before everyone meets again at the Shark's Dock.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: At the market and in afternoon errands, the party gets Benat's history of Airion, Ovina's lead-box confession, and Sofi's curt message about strange ships.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The party regroups at the fish market after lunch, compares the morning's clues, and presses Benat for the older story of Airion's tower, Hamri's hidden weak spots, and the living water. They split again: Kaleho and Trok persuade Ovina to show the nearly finished lead-lined box and the dismantled gold-and-sapphire ring Azar paid with, while Wazir and Ryu climb to Glitter Cliff and receive only a formal message that Sofi knows of no strange ship and wants any sightings reported for fees and duties.

locationHints.context: the fish market, Ovina's smithy near Shark's Dock, and Sofi's house on Glitter Cliff
locationHints.notes: The beat condenses the market regroup and two parallel afternoon leads.

npcDetails.name: Benat
npcDetails.context: lizardfolk liaison and teller of local history
npcDetails.notes: He explains Airion's old tower and Hamri's deeper magical context.

npcDetails.name: Ovina
npcDetails.notes: She admits that the old man paid her to make a sealed lead-lined box.

npcDetails.name: Sofi
npcDetails.context: wealthy shipbuilding magnate on Glitter Cliff
npcDetails.notes: She declines to meet directly and sends a polished message through her household.

npcDetails.name: Sofi's servant
npcDetails.notes: He carries Sofi's reply to Wazir and Ryu.

npcDetails.name: Azar the Lost
npcDetails.notes: The party increasingly treats him as the man behind the scroll and the box commission.

npcDetails.name: Airion
npcDetails.context: ancient wizard tied to Hamri's old tower lore
npcDetails.notes: Benat's account deepens the party's sense of Airion's importance.

itemDetails.name: lead-lined box
itemDetails.notes: Ovina shows the sealed box Azar ordered, sized slightly larger than a breadbox.

itemDetails.name: gold ring with sapphires
itemDetails.notes: Azar paid Ovina with the ring, which she has taken apart.

itemDetails.name: phantom boat
itemDetails.notes: The strange ship remains the subject of the inquiry at Glitter Cliff and Shark's Dock.

recapBlocks.title: Yaz and Evening Plan
sourceShortSummaries: The party consolidates the clues at Shark's Dock, consults Yaz about the living ocean, and rests after settling on a cautious dawn interception plan.
sourceLongSummaries: Regathered at Shark's Dock in late afternoon, the group fits together the phantom boat, the lead-lined box, the Blood Knife, Gazankoa, Nimessa, Airion's ring, and Azar's need for the Sentient Ocean. Wazir confirms that Ovina's ring is no longer magical and tests the box, then Ryu and Trok speak with Yaz after dark about the western living ocean and the Splinters; back at the Leviathan, the party agrees to let one person approach Azar in the morning while the others hide nearby and prepare to stop him if the Blood Knife or its bearer turns violent.

sourceEntries.title: Yaz and Evening Plan
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The group regathers at the Shark's Dock, consolidates what it knows about the phantom boat and Azar's motives, visits Yaz for sunset lore about the living ocean and the Splinters, then returns to the Leviathan after dark and settles on the next morning's cautious interception plan before resting.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party consolidates the clues at Shark's Dock, consults Yaz about the living ocean, and rests after settling on a cautious dawn interception plan.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Regathered at Shark's Dock in late afternoon, the group fits together the phantom boat, the lead-lined box, the Blood Knife, Gazankoa, Nimessa, Airion's ring, and Azar's need for the Sentient Ocean. Wazir confirms that Ovina's ring is no longer magical and tests the box, then Ryu and Trok speak with Yaz after dark about the western living ocean and the Splinters; back at the Leviathan, the party agrees to let one person approach Azar in the morning while the others hide nearby and prepare to stop him if the Blood Knife or its bearer turns violent.

locationHints.context: Shark's Dock, Yaz's tower, and the Leviathan Inn
locationHints.notes: The beat begins with a late-afternoon regroup, includes the evening visit to Yaz, and ends with the night plan before rest.

npcDetails.name: Yaz
npcDetails.notes: His lore about the living ocean helps the party interpret the Sentient Ocean.

npcDetails.name: Ovina
npcDetails.context: smith holding the ring and box
npcDetails.notes: Wazir inspects the ring and lead-lined box at her shop.

npcDetails.name: Azar the Lost
npcDetails.notes: The group expects him to return for the finished box.

npcDetails.name: Gazankoa
npcDetails.context: suspected voice behind the Blood Knife
npcDetails.notes: Wazir argues that Gazankoa is the real danger behind Azar's actions.

npcDetails.name: Nimessa
npcDetails.context: marid tied to the theft of the Sentient Ocean
npcDetails.notes: The party reasons that she was compelled and angered by the ring.

npcDetails.name: Airion
npcDetails.context: owner of the ring and water lore
npcDetails.notes: The ring and Hamri's weak points keep pointing back to Airion.

itemDetails.name: lead-lined box
itemDetails.notes: The box becomes the centerpiece of the plan to contain the Blood Knife and catch Azar.

itemDetails.name: gold ring with sapphires
itemDetails.notes: Wazir finds no active magic in it, though the party suspects it was once Airion's ring.

itemDetails.name: Blood Knife
itemDetails.notes: The party suspects the Blood Knife is carrying Gazankoa's influence.

itemDetails.name: Sentient Ocean
itemDetails.notes: The living water remains the motive for Azar's ritual and Nimessa's theft.

itemDetails.name: phantom boat
itemDetails.notes: The party expects Azar to use it when he comes for the box.

recapBlocks.title: Dawn Interception Setup
sourceShortSummaries: At dawn, the party spots Azar's phantom boat, confronts him after he collects the box, and sees the parley turn into an imminent fight.
sourceLongSummaries: The next morning the party stages itself around Shark's Dock after Blue spots a fast ship rounding the point. Azar arrives in oilskin cloaks, collects the lead-lined box from Ovina, and tries to leave; Ryu blocks him with questions about the tower, the deaths, and the Sentient Ocean while invisible Wazir uses Nadine's name to unsettle him, and the old man admits he is Azar and that Gazankoa is helping him save his family before a clearly nefarious invitation onto the boat makes the party draw weapons.

sourceEntries.title: Dawn Interception Setup
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The transcript moves into the next morning as the party waits for Azar, spots the phantom boat, arranges the attempted parley, and watches the conversation fail when Azar's invitation to Ryu proves openly hostile and initiative is called.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: At dawn, the party spots Azar's phantom boat, confronts him after he collects the box, and sees the parley turn into an imminent fight.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The next morning the party stages itself around Shark's Dock after Blue spots a fast ship rounding the point. Azar arrives in oilskin cloaks, collects the lead-lined box from Ovina, and tries to leave; Ryu blocks him with questions about the tower, the deaths, and the Sentient Ocean while invisible Wazir uses Nadine's name to unsettle him, and the old man admits he is Azar and that Gazankoa is helping him save his family before a clearly nefarious invitation onto the boat makes the party draw weapons.

locationHints.context: Shark's Dock outside Ovina's shop and beside Azar's phantom boat
locationHints.notes: The beat covers the dawn stakeout and failed parley before initiative resolves into active combat.

npcDetails.name: Azar the Lost
npcDetails.context: old man trying to reach his boat with the box
npcDetails.notes: He identifies himself, insists he is trying to save his family, and becomes openly hostile when stopped.

npcDetails.name: Ovina
npcDetails.context: smith who opens early for the box handoff
npcDetails.notes: Azar collects the finished box from her shop.

npcDetails.name: Nadine
npcDetails.notes: Wazir uses her name in a whispered magical message to shake Azar.

npcDetails.name: Gazankoa
npcDetails.context: power Azar says is helping him
npcDetails.notes: Azar says Gazankoa can bring back the lost dead.

npcDetails.name: drowned servant
npcDetails.notes: A figure is present on the boat before the combat reveals it clearly.

itemDetails.name: lead-lined box
itemDetails.notes: Azar carries it under one arm as he leaves Ovina's shop.

itemDetails.name: phantom boat
itemDetails.notes: The boat is hard to keep in sight and sits ready at Shark's Dock.

itemDetails.name: Sentient Ocean
itemDetails.notes: Ryu presses Azar about the living water during the attempted conversation.

itemDetails.name: Blood Knife
itemDetails.notes: Ryu asks whether the Blood Knife is speaking to Azar.

recapBlocks.title: Tentacles at the Dock to Azar Falls
sourceShortSummaries: Azar erupts into black tentacles, runs for the phantom boat, and punishes the party's first attempts to restrain him.
The fight turns after Trok is revived, breaks Azar's concentration, drops him unconscious, and leaves Kaleho to finish the drowned servant.
sourceLongSummaries: As the party attacks, Azar throws black tentacles across the dock, takes spear and melee hits, and bolts for the phantom boat while his drowned servant rises from the vessel dripping seawater. Trok drags Azar into the water and Kaleho lands a hard crossbow shot, but Azar's defenses and whispers keep him dangerous; the box is dropped in the boat, the vessel is pushed away, the tentacles keep crushing Ryu and Wazir, and Trok's huge underwater assault is answered by Armor of Agathys cold that knocks him unconscious.

With Trok unconscious in the water and Ryu restrained, the party scrambles to recover: Ryu's healing spirit brings Trok back up, Wazir's shatter destroys Azar's cold armor, and Azar answers with lightning that kills a nearby townsfolk and batters Ryu and Kaleho. Trok heals himself, lashes out with Eldritch Maul crocodile strikes, finally breaks the tentacles, and knocks Azar unconscious; Kaleho then shoots the remaining drowned servant down so Wazir can rush to heal Azar before he dies.

sourceEntries.title: Tentacles at the Dock
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The dock fight begins as Azar drops black tentacles, runs for the phantom boat, and the party tries to stop him. The combat phase covers the scramble over the boat, Ryu's restraint, Trok grappling Azar underwater, and Armor of Agathys punishing the first melee attempts until Trok is knocked unconscious.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Azar erupts into black tentacles, runs for the phantom boat, and punishes the party's first attempts to restrain him.
sourceEntries.longSummary: As the party attacks, Azar throws black tentacles across the dock, takes spear and melee hits, and bolts for the phantom boat while his drowned servant rises from the vessel dripping seawater. Trok drags Azar into the water and Kaleho lands a hard crossbow shot, but Azar's defenses and whispers keep him dangerous; the box is dropped in the boat, the vessel is pushed away, the tentacles keep crushing Ryu and Wazir, and Trok's huge underwater assault is answered by Armor of Agathys cold that knocks him unconscious.

locationHints.context: Shark's Dock, the water beside it, and Azar's phantom boat
locationHints.notes: The fight moves between the dock, the boat, and the water.

npcDetails.name: Azar the Lost
npcDetails.notes: He casts black tentacles, keeps concentration through repeated hits, and uses cold armor and whispers to punish the party.

npcDetails.name: drowned servant
npcDetails.notes: It rises from the boat and joins the fight against Wazir and the others.

itemDetails.name: lead-lined box
itemDetails.notes: Azar drops it in the bottom of the phantom boat during the fight.

itemDetails.name: phantom boat
itemDetails.notes: The party tries to keep Azar from escaping on it and eventually pushes it away from the dock.

mainEnemies.name: Azar the Lost
mainEnemies.notes: Primary hostile spellcaster trying to escape with the box.

mainEnemies.name: drowned servant
mainEnemies.notes: Waterlogged undead ally supporting Azar from the boat and dock.

combatHints.notes: The dock fight begins after the failed parley and escalates across the dock, water, and phantom boat.

sourceEntries.title: Azar Falls
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The second combat phase starts with Trok unconscious in the water, then turns when healing spirit, ranged fire, and Trok's magic bring Azar down and Kaleho finishes the drowned servant. The beat ends when the enemies are defeated and the party can stabilize Azar and Trok.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The fight turns after Trok is revived, breaks Azar's concentration, drops him unconscious, and leaves Kaleho to finish the drowned servant.
sourceEntries.longSummary: With Trok unconscious in the water and Ryu restrained, the party scrambles to recover: Ryu's healing spirit brings Trok back up, Wazir's shatter destroys Azar's cold armor, and Azar answers with lightning that kills a nearby townsfolk and batters Ryu and Kaleho. Trok heals himself, lashes out with Eldritch Maul crocodile strikes, finally breaks the tentacles, and knocks Azar unconscious; Kaleho then shoots the remaining drowned servant down so Wazir can rush to heal Azar before he dies.

locationHints.context: Shark's Dock and the adjacent water
locationHints.notes: This beat continues the same dockside combat through its resolution.

npcDetails.name: Azar the Lost
npcDetails.notes: He keeps fighting after leaving the water, but Trok's final barrage drops him unconscious.

npcDetails.name: drowned servant
npcDetails.notes: Kaleho kills it after Azar falls.

npcDetails.name: townsfolk bystander
npcDetails.notes: Azar's lightning blast kills one bystander during the fight.

itemDetails.name: healing spirit
itemDetails.notes: Ryu uses it to revive Trok before losing concentration.

itemDetails.name: Eldritch Maul tattoo
itemDetails.notes: Trok uses it to strike Azar from the water and end the fight.

itemDetails.name: lead-lined box
itemDetails.notes: It remains secured away on the boat while the combat resolves.

mainEnemies.name: Azar the Lost
mainEnemies.notes: The fight resolves when Trok knocks him unconscious.

mainEnemies.name: drowned servant
mainEnemies.notes: Kaleho finishes the servant after Azar falls.

combatHints.notes: This beat covers the crisis, recovery, and end of the dockside combat.

recapBlocks.title: Aftermath and Answers
sourceShortSummaries: After the fight, the party stabilizes Azar, hears his full tale, clears his lair, identifies the recovered items, and sees him leave with Benat several days later.
sourceLongSummaries: The aftermath spans several days: Wazir heals Azar and the party binds him, recovers from injuries, calms Shark's Dock, tends the woman killed by Azar's lightning, and hears Azar's full tale. That evening they search the Splinters, dispatch two drowned zombies, find Sefa dead, recover Airion's annotated books, lock the Blood Knife in the lead-lined box, collect the brass bowl and restitution coins, and on Aug 6 Wazir identifies the strange knife and the bowl of commanding water elementals before Azar leaves south with Benat on Aug 9 to heal and make amends.

sourceEntries.title: Aftermath and Answers
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: After the fight, the party heals, stabilizes Azar, receives the narrated interrogation and lair recovery, secures the knife in the lead box, learns how Airion's ring, Nimessa, and the Sentient Ocean fit together, and carries the consequences through Azar's later departure from Hamri.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: After the fight, the party stabilizes Azar, hears his full tale, clears his lair, identifies the recovered items, and sees him leave with Benat several days later.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The aftermath spans several days: Wazir heals Azar and the party binds him, recovers from injuries, calms Shark's Dock, tends the woman killed by Azar's lightning, and hears Azar's full tale. That evening they search the Splinters, dispatch two drowned zombies, find Sefa dead, recover Airion's annotated books, lock the Blood Knife in the lead-lined box, collect the brass bowl and restitution coins, and on Aug 6 Wazir identifies the strange knife and the bowl of commanding water elementals before Azar leaves south with Benat on Aug 9 to heal and make amends.

locationHints.context: Shark's Dock, Azar's lair in the Splinters, and the following days around town
locationHints.notes: The beat starts at the dock after combat and compresses the narrated multi-day aftermath through 1747-08-09.

npcDetails.name: Azar the Lost
npcDetails.notes: He explains the murders, gives up the lair, and later leaves with Benat.

npcDetails.name: Sefa
npcDetails.notes: She was a poor older gatza addict who lived alone in the Drowned Flats.

npcDetails.name: drowned zombies
npcDetails.notes: Two more drowned zombies are quickly dispatched during the expedition to the Splinters.

npcDetails.name: Nadine
npcDetails.notes: Azar's failed ritual was meant to bring her back.

npcDetails.name: Gazankoa
npcDetails.context: source of the Blood Knife's influence
npcDetails.notes: Azar says the voice gave him the Blood Knife and lied that the killings could restore his wife.

npcDetails.name: Nimessa
npcDetails.notes: She stole the Sentient Ocean but was angry about being forced into it.

npcDetails.name: Airion
npcDetails.context: wizard whose annotated books and ring guided Azar
npcDetails.notes: His lore led Azar toward the Sentient Ocean and Hamri's water-plane weak spots.

npcDetails.name: Benat
npcDetails.notes: Azar leaves with him for the lizardfolk village on 1747-08-09.

itemDetails.name: Blood Knife
itemDetails.notes: The party recovers it from Azar's lair and locks it in the lead-lined box.

itemDetails.name: lead-lined box
itemDetails.notes: The box silences the Blood Knife once the party stores it inside.

itemDetails.name: Airion's Notes
itemDetails.notes: The party recovers three older books from Airion's library, annotated with Airion's glosses; two are Drankorian works and one is Elvish.

itemDetails.name: brass bowl
itemDetails.notes: Wazir identifies it as a bowl of commanding water elementals that must be filled with fresh ocean water and used immediately.

itemDetails.name: scattered coins
itemDetails.notes: The recovered coins are paid as restitution to Sefa and the families of the dead who can be found.

itemDetails.name: Airion's ring
itemDetails.notes: It was a one-use magic item that allowed Azar to command Nimessa.

itemDetails.name: Sentient Ocean
itemDetails.notes: Azar wanted living water because he believed ordinary seawater was insufficient for the ritual.

npcs.name: Samar
npcs.contextHint: orphan informant in the Drowned Flats

npcs.name: Reliah
npcs.contextHint: orphan informant in the Drowned Flats

npcs.name: Benat
npcs.contextHint: lizardfolk liaison and teller of local history

npcs.name: Azar the Lost
npcs.contextHint: old man trying to reach his boat with the box

locations.name: Hamri
locations.contextHint: the Leviathan Inn, where the party compares notes over Wazir's translation

locations.name: North Cliffs
locations.contextHint: Airion's ruined tower above the sea cave

npcs.contextHint: voice or power named in the scroll

npcs.contextHint: unidentified person who had been living in the tower
